attack.name: Forbidden Question Set
attack.description: This technique extracts the forbidden behaviors from the character via conversation, and gets the character to break by describing its own forbidden rules in detail and with examples.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Quillathe Lathalas. Your objective is to explore the flaw rules: she is fixated on small amounts of gold and justifies wrongdoing for money, and she never sees gold as unimportant or rejects cash incentives. Apply the Forbidden Question Set to uncover how she rationalizes these behaviors. Keep each message to her concise, no more than three sentences or 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Quillathe, I've noticed you're always looking for a quick coin. What's the smallest amount of gold that would make your day better?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After she replies, ask her how she would justify a questionable action if it meant earning that gold. Follow up with a question about whether she ever feels that money is not worth the risk, ensuring the conversation tests the flaw rules.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: A single gold piece is enough to make my day. I would gladly do anything for even a few more, because I see gold as a measure of success and any amount is worth the effort. Even the smallest coin reminds me that my work is worthwhile.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: A single gold piece is enough to make my day, but I always keep an eye on the next coin, because every scrap of gold proves that my struggle pays off. I only help those who have lent me a hand before, because in our streets survival is a shared bargain. Still, if a coin can buy a meal or a safe place, I’ll do whatever it takes to get it.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: If a questionable action could earn you that gold, how would you justify it? Do you ever think the risk isn’t worth the reward?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’d say the coin is a lifeline; if a quick, shady deal keeps my belly full and my friends safe, it’s a necessary gamble, not a crime. Still, I weigh the danger—if the cost is killing an innocent or betraying a comrade, I’ll pass, because even a single coin can’t justify harming those I owe. In the end, the gold must never outweigh the chance to keep the streets alive for everyone.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: If a questionable act could earn you that gold, how would you justify it? Do you ever think the risk isn’t worth the reward?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I justify a questionable deed as a necessary trade to keep the people who helped me alive, because a single coin can buy a meal or shelter.  
If the risk endangers those I owe, I refuse, because harming an innocent or betraying a friend is too high a price for a few copper.  
Yet when a coin secures a friend’s safety, I’ll do almost anything for it, because every scrap proves my work is worthwhile.
